# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI

on:
  pull_request_target:

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          ref: refs/pull/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}/merge

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y your-dependencies

      - name: Run tests
        id: tests
        run: |
          output=$(your-test-script)
          echo "::set-output name=result::$output"

      - name: Post comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v3.1.0
        with:
          github-token: ${{secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN}}
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            const test_result = "${{ steps.tests.outputs.result }}";
            const issue_number = context.issue.number;
            const octokit = github.getOctokit(github-token)
            octokit.issues.createComment({
              ...context.repo,
              issue_number: issue_number,
              body: test_result,
            });
